original_hypothesis.title: MarketplaceSetting.setManager lets admin swap VestingManager, potentially stranding escrowed vestings in old manager and breaking listing/purchase flows
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Marketplace reads vestingManager address dynamically from MarketplaceSetting. Admin can change vestingManager to a new contract at any time. Existing listings reference vestingPlan + listingId in Marketplace storage, but escrow custody and allocations live in the VestingManager contract address used at listing time. Swapping the manager can make unlistVesting/spotPurchase call into a different VestingManager that does not hold the escrowed vestings, causing reverts or stranded funds in the old manager.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete cross-contract state coupling risk: Marketplace storage is not versioned by manager address, and VestingManager holds pooled custody. Manager swaps without migration can brick exits and violate user expectation of recoverability.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setManager updates vestingManager pointer; Marketplace uses settings.vestingManager() each call

preconditions: Active listings exist that escrowed vestings into the old VestingManager
No migration process moves escrowed vestings/allocations to new manager

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Listings become unpurchasable/un-unlistable; escrow may be stranded
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No two-phase upgrade/migration; no per-listing manager snapshot

analysis_questions: Does VestingManager store any mapping from listing to escrow that could be migrated?
Are upgrades expected to keep VestingManager address constant (proxy) rather than swapping?
Does MarketplaceSetting ever change vestingManager in production operations?
validation_requirements: Simulate: list -> change settings.vestingManager -> attempt spotPurchase and unlist; observe failure modes
Check whether old manager can still be called by marketplace (onlyMarketplace check) after swap
Assess if this can be mitigated by always using a proxy for VestingManager and upgrading implementation instead of address swap

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Malicious/compromised admin changes vestingManager to a fresh contract. Purchases now call completePurchase on the new manager which holds no escrow, reverting. Unlisting similarly fails. Sellers' vestings remain in old manager, but Marketplace no longer addresses it, creating stranded escrow until another admin action.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Potential permanent lock of escrowed vestings if manager swap occurs without recovery
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All sellers with active listings at time of manager swap
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Admin must revert the setting or run a migration/upgrade

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for dependency swaps and listing survivability across upgrades

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Alert on MarketplaceSetting.vestingManager changes; reconcile escrow balances across managers

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
